title: Is Food Addiction Related to Poor Gut Health?

chunks.title: Dietary Discipline and Sugar Sensitivity
chunks.summary: The video begins by addressing the difficulties some individuals face in maintaining discipline on a diet, particularly when consuming foods high in sugar. The speaker suggests that while limiting carbohydrates may be beneficial for some, it is likely that underlying gut issues are also a significant factor for those experiencing adverse reactions to sugars like fruit and honey.
chunks.transcript: I know there are some people out there who feel like they have difficulty maintaining discipline on a diet and that when they eat foods with sugar even fruit or fruit juice their blood sugar spikes and they lose control now in these situations I think there's an argument to be made for limiting carbohydrates but I think that this is the minority of people out there and I think there's probably something else going on that is causing people to feel that way that may need correction

chunks.title: Identifying Gut Issues
chunks.summary: The speaker shares an example from a podcast conversation regarding a person who has gut reactions to fruit, later diagnosed with small intestinal bacterial overgrowth (SIBO). It emphasizes that such adverse reactions are likely related to gut health rather than fruit itself, highlighting the importance of addressing these underlying issues.
chunks.transcript: there's a good example that I'm reminded of here which is that when I was in Los Angeles I did a podcast with Mari Luellen and she doesn't have food addiction but she doesn't have good GI reactions to fruit and my sense was that she had dpois sometimes known as sibo small intestinal bacterial overgrowth which is really misnomer and in fact when she did get tested she did end up having sibo dispos and so for those of you who don't react well to Honey or fruit or are sensitive to fod Maps quote unquote fod maps that is a gut issue primarily that is not an issue with fruit and honey that is a gut issue that needs to get resolved in my belief and

chunks.title: The Role of Raw Dairy in Gut Health
chunks.summary: The speaker advocates for the inclusion of raw dairy as a remedy for gut issues, arguing that it has significant biological value and can aid in improving lactose tolerance. They discuss personal experiences and anecdotal evidence supporting the notion that raw dairy can help individuals regain the ability to metabolize lactose effectively.
chunks.transcript: I've come to believe that the best way to resolve that gut issue is with something like raw dery I've done another recent podcast on raw dery and a reason that I think it's so essentially criminal that raw dairy is illegal in many states or not easily obtained in many states recently talking to a friend in Australia New South Wales and she was telling me that raw milk is illegal there I've spoken to people in Canada raw milk is illegal in Canada that's a subject for a different podcasts but I think there are it's it's criminal that I healing food for humans that has so much biological Integrity so much biological value is illegal in parts of the world I think that talks to broader issues about where we are as humans but I want to reemphasize the point so that many of you understand this I believe that if you have reactions fruit and honey fix your gun and I think the simplest way to think about that is fix your gut by including raw dairy If you're lactose intolerant start slowly use a fermented raw dairy like a cfir and your body will almost invariably begin to metabolize the lactose on its own that's been my experience that's been the experience of so chunks.title: Gut Microbiome and Reactions to Dairy
chunks.summary: This segment addresses the potential for negative reactions when first introducing raw dairy or colostrum, such as bloating or skin flare-ups, due to shifts in gut microbiome. The speaker reassures viewers that these reactions can be indicative of beneficial changes in gut health and suggests methods to manage discomfort during this adjustment phase.
chunks.transcript: you're shifting your gut microbiome or you believe you have disbiosis AKA sibo is that there may be some degree of die off as you begin to shift your gut microbiome I've had a couple of friends tell me that when they drink colostrum or that when they take hardened soil grass-fed colostrum supplements that they get bloating and to me this is an indication that the bacteria the immunoglobulins in the colostrum the immunoactive components the biological components of the colossum are doing something and if any of you have ever taken a probiotic and gotten diarrhea from taking a probiotic you understand that when you introduce new bacteria or new immunologic components immunoglobulins that are concentrated in colostrum there is often a die off if there's an incompatibility between those populations so this is how you shift your gut Flora drinking yogurt or I would say raw yogurt especially raw cafee or raw dairy some people have worsening of skin issues immediately when they do this and then it gradually improves over time and I think this is probably a flare potentially related to inflammation die off of bacteria leading to break down of the cell membrane endotoxin AKA lipop polysaccharide so understand that shifting your gut Flora is not always easy and there may be some uphill before you get to the downhill

chunks.title: Healing and Managing Gut Discomfort
chunks.summary: Finally, the speaker discusses strategies for managing discomfort associated with introducing raw dairy into the diet, such as using activated charcoal. They reiterate the importance of understanding one's gut responses and the potential for adjusting dietary habits in a natural and evolutionary context.
chunks.transcript: but that not every reaction to a raw dairy whether it's bloating slightly flare in the skin issues is a bad thing sometimes that happens when you have a die off and and I think that the way to combat that most simply is to take something like charcoal so we're kind of off path now on the fruit juice but I think this is a valuable tangent and I just want to share this with you all that if you have gut issues if you can't tolerate fruit and honey and you believe you have dispos and this is almost something you can diagnose symptomatically you don't need fancy testing to do this but you can do fancy testing if you would like to corroborate this idea doing raw da I think is the simplest path there the most naturalistic path the path that makes the most sense evolutionarily but if the raw dairy is then triggering for you or causing GI issues take it with some
